reviews.review_title: Worth it I wish there was a DLC
reviews.review_description: My husband and I have searched all over to find a VR escape room worth playing and this is by far the best one available. The price does seem a little steep at 20 dollars but you get your moneys worth. We have solved two of them so far and halfway through a third one before we ran out of time. I will say the one with the pharaoh is about impossible we have played that one numerous times and always run out of time before we can get through the final room. I feel like that one is a little too hard or maybe just too many steps with the amount of time you have to do it in but other than that we really enjoy this game and wish the Developers would make a DLC or two to add more rooms to the game!

It’s good!! It makes your brain work. 
I’m not sure cuz I haven’t done them all yet but it seems like the difficulty goes up with every room? Room one was the easiest so far. 
I only wish I could stop the timer and just continue. Room 3 took me 3 hours it’s a bit annoying that I have to redo all the same things over again just to continue. On the other hand it might make sense if you co-op but I’m doing it solo. There should be an option for not so smart people haha. 
Otherwise it’s really really great.

Update: taking a few stars down for timing. Puzzles are not easy, they take time, every quest has multiple rooms and once you think you’re done , nope, there’s more and more rooms, there are some spots that take me the whole hour and having to come back again and again and to start over to find out there one more room is absolutely annoying. The should beam option to switch that timer off.

Unless you have an IQ Einstein would be jelous of , dont bother... Im not daft by any means and have played this with multiple people ranging for mentally slow to very clever... The puzzles lack any logic whatsoever and it becomes very boring very quickly...Gaming is meant to be fun and emersive... this is anything but.

The unique places make it all the more fun. Each adventure is new and exciting. Its great with multiple players so where one lacks knowledge on something, someone else picks up what's needed to solve puzzles.  The only negative I have, I wish they would make more!!!

reviews.review_title: I was disconnected so many time
reviews.review_description: Everytime I play with my friends, it usually be disconnected although wifi still ok...I dont get it, how can I fix it

I am a seasoned escape room player with over 15 escape rooms solved at various locations and this is the most realistic escape room game out there. There are so many people whining about the timer but it’s the whole part of an escape room so if you can’t solve ad you complain about it go back to the baby jigsaw puzzle. But please make more rooms.

Includes the worst game ever - tile/sliding puzzle. Why??? One might resort to that when they are out of creative ideas. And a timer??? Why would you design an entire game and then rush people through it?  The first time I had to start over because of that stupid sliding puzzle, I was done. Refund please.
These are very nice. 1 hr would be perfect BUT MAKE MORE

With the timer, the game makes no fun. To repeat a room several times is just annoying. Those tile puzzles are no fun, either, several minutes of moving around the puzzle tiles... Because of that timer, only one star, it really destroys the fun.

This is one of the best games my husband and I have had the pleasure of enjoying together! Hours of fun working together and maybe a few moments arguing lol. Don’t pay attention to any of the negative reviews. This game is amazing and I’m really hoping for more rooms to be added. Pretty please!!!

My boyfriend and I really enjoyed the games! Yes they were tough, yes we had to start over as we ran out of time but that's what happens with escape rooms. Yes, real escape rooms let you have hints but come on people. It was a lot of fun. My only wish is that there were more chapters. 
Thanks for making me jump and scream and use my brain to figure out tough tasks. 
If you make more I will definitely buy!!

Ooo wow, this one is good. And with some horror details. 
Just did a 1st run. Didn’t finished on time. Have to restart. But now I now already some puzzles.
Please add more content

reviews.review_title: We loved it, want more rooms!
reviews.review_description:
Challenging, frustrating but addictive. Timer can be annoying but it’s good for a break. Besides, don’t want to finish too quickly or it’s over. When playing in a pair, divide and conquer worked best.
More rooms! More rooms please!

I love Escape Rooms, both in VR and real life.  In a real life Escape Room -- which you say you want to emulate -- it's true there's usually a timer.  But they ALSO have a robust Hint System, where you can get a hint or two from staff (or otherwise) to help you get through a stuck point.  In VR, the other great Escape Rooms I've played (e.g. The Room VR is among the best) allow for a Save Game system.  Both features cut down on the frustration for users generally, since they may come to the game with different skill levels and puzzle-solving approaches.

Your product -- while otherwise impressive -- has neither.  This is baffling.  Because  even with a quick read through the first screen of Reviews I counted at least 5 people who have strongly suggested you remove the Timer feature, and/or allow Save Games.  Yet you persist in defending the position that you want to "recreate" a real-life Escape Room experience.  In my case, I have sadly given up because the stress / pressure of trying to finish before time runs out is NOT what I play for.   

As these Reviews begging for no Timer pile up, they will discourage other buyers of your otherwise-excellent game.  Sad for everyone.  I suggest you listen with a more open mind. The drumbeat is getting louder.

Developer defends their “time limit” for “repeatability”. Nah dawg. I would spend time in these rooms forever but if you’re gonna give me cryptic puzzles and a time limit there’s a certain point where if you get stuck too long you can’t recover. That’s not good that’s just poor game design, I’m not going back to replay the level because I got stuck on a puzzle for a tad too long. No thanks. Enjoy your 1 star for the amount of times I’ll play this mess.

reviews.review_title: it could be a great game
reviews.review_description:
I love escape room games and the rooms are really well made.
i don't really like playing games with jump scars but i can stand it because i really like this game.
Like many others, I would like to do it without a time limit.  
I don't mind to see the video for the lost ending if you have not finished it in time, but you should be able to go back to the room to finish it, without having to start again. Especially as some puzzles needs a little bit of luck.
What has happened to me a few times, is that I dropped something and couldn't pick it up again and had to restart the room. Of course unhappy if you are almost done with the room as you are not able to safe.
Nevertheless, the game is great and really fun.

Game leans towards a creepy/horror vibe which isn't my preference, and has 2 Aztec themed levels which seems redundant when there are only 5 levels. I wish they had an easy mode. I have to use walkthroughs because it's too hard for me and my partner, and it's not like "oh yeah, I should've figured that out" it's like "how on Earth was I supposed to figure that out?" There are things I don't understand even after knowing the answer. Many parts of the puzzles could be easily transformed into a simpler version for people who want something more casual. Or without an easy mode, it would really be nice if there were a hint system, like in real escape rooms. It REALLY decreases the fun when I have to look up a walkthrough on YouTube on my phone while keeping my headset on.

I agree with others that there should be an option for no timer. I personally do not enjoy the timer and it is unnecessary for a game, as opposed to a real life Escape Room which has hard costs associated with each minute played. People are paying for your game and they have a right to play the game they want to play it, provided it can be coded in easily. An option to remove the timer is essentially no work, and would allow customers to play the way they prefer to play.

Having said that, it's ~10 hours of moderately fun gameplay for $20. I'm still satisfied with the purchase, but I would have preferred 5 hours of very fun gameplay, AKA hints and/or easy mode and no timer.

I’ll be honest I liked this game in the beginning and I think it has great potential.  But I have spent nearly 12 hours trying to figure out the stupid sarcophagus puzzle.  I have yet have have anything move.  The in game help is of no help.  It just makes me angry that it is illogical.  The boxes move backwards.  Just very frustrated.  I wanted to relax not be vexed .  Update the game to give better information!

reviews.review_title: Good game with a few cons
reviews.review_description:
PROS: It captures what a scape room is + Lots of cleaver puzzles + Good graphics and atmosphere + Varied scenarios

CONS: Some grab problems + If the time is running out you'll have to do all over again and in some rooms it's very very tight (i'd add a timeless or extra time option)
Love this game.  Challenging puzzles, decent graphics so much fun online with friends
If you lose connection you should be able to join back in the room you were working on with the room code.

reviews.review_title: Amazing VR Horror Co-op Escape Rooms
reviews.review_description:
This app does an AMAZING job of simulating a series of horror-themed escape rooms. It is one of the very few escape room VR apps that lets you work in tandem with another player, as you solve the puzzles together. My wife and I love real life escape rooms (our current count is 152 as of this review) and the five rooms in Another Door make us feel just like we have in some of our favorite IRL rooms. The creepy vibes of each room add to the drama and tension as well. In one room, we got a jump scare so bad my wife literally fell to the ground out of fright. 

If you search for Escape Room on the Meta Store, you will get a plethora of results. Unfortunately, you'll find most of them to not be worth your time and certainly not your money. Another Door is the rare exception. The rooms in this app are WELL worth your time and your money. If they ever release a sequel or any DLC rooms, I'll buy them instantly.

The mix of rooms is sweet – from the creepy/scary to the bloody to ancient mysteries.
...
Pro: difficulty is not too hard (but may require a second or third try) , great fun in Co-Op , puzzles adjust (different in Solo vs. Co-Op) , good mix of puzzles and required problem-solving strategies.
... Contra: without a supply of new rooms (even DLC), once completed, the game has little to no replay-value.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your gameplay experience with us. 
We'll keep working to bring more fun escape room experiences to VR game players.

reviews.review_description: The timer ruins this game, its a good puzzle game but the time limit ruins the whole experience. If they put an option to remove time limit ill come back to this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your gameplay experience with us. 
The time limit is a rule we've implemented to encourage repeat play. We're listening to your feedback and will pass it on to our development team.

reviews.review_description: I had so much grin with my friend solving these rooms. We finished the 5 escape room in 5 days. So worth the money. Love the different rooms and that it was challenging with little clues. Made it so worth it. I want another one now.
Meta  needs more co-op / multiplayer escape rooms like this.

Purchased this game so my husband and I could vr together. He gets motion sickness but I don’t, so the different movement options are awesome. The games are creepy and can be challenging. I love finding/solving the extra puzzles for the optional artifacts in each room — it gives each level a lot of replayability. The rooms change slightly if you’re playing alone or with someone else. Teamwork is required in multiplayer whereas solo requires only you! I appreciated that the developers decorated the manor for Christmas and even had Christmas music playing lol great game, hope they do another.

This game is so well done! A friend and I worked through every room and redid them solo to get all of the collectibles. Every room is unique and challenging. I'm really hoping for more content. Congrats to the developers on this amazing success!

Loved Dark Room VR and have been waiting for another game and this one popped up. Thought I would give it a try have barely gotten into it, but I can tell this is a great game. It is a little difficult and a hint button would be great maybe allowing so many hints per room? Or adding a feature where you can turn the clock off so that you have as much time as you want to clear the room would be great for the players like me who just wanna take our time and have fun playing the game other than that the game is great and a lot of fun! I also love that this is one of the first games like this that has a multi player function. That is a huge plus.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your interest in our game. we didn't include a save system because our concept is to recreate the experience of a real-life escape in VR. However, we're always listening to your feedback and will work on improving it.

reviews.review_title: Need to be able to turn timer off
reviews.review_description: I'm not a big puzzle gamer and not much of a fan but enjoy these every now and then, bad news this is a timed event which would be nice if u could turn the timer off and take ur time to finish it , with no progress save kills the motivation to even want to beat the clock,typical escape room so no progress is saved if u run out of time which happens. Kinda kills a game as I'm not about to redo 75 percent of a puzzle room over again to play the last part. So on to the next room to repeat same progress. End up playing all rooms for one run only timer goes off end of room. Very difficult in solo better with 2 much better with more. Seriously this is not a solo game . If ever progress was saved these games would be worth finishing a room but some puzzles like the pic scramble is extemely boring and troublesome to do over. If only it had at least one auto save per room 2 even better otherwise it's not worth more than 10 bucks at the most as a short cheap experience. Shatter is way better and the best in MR mixed reality.  Amazing game, unfortunately it has no saves either kinda lazy development as ppl have complained about it. But I prefer sitting have to stand again feels lazy not to add it with auto saves.

developer_response.body: Thank you for purchasing our game. We intentionally did not include a mid-save mode at launch to provide a real life escape room experience. We also designed the gimmicks to be fun for two or more players to experience together, and many of the gimmicks require you to stand up to solve, so unfortunately we were unable to include a sit-down mode. We appreciate your feedback and will discuss it with our developers to make your experience better.

reviews.review_description:
I realize this is new, so hopefully there is much more to come. My biggest complaints  are the mechanics....not actually putting a key in a lock and turning it rather than having to hover over something and let go ( like taking a knife and cutting rope for instance) is beyond annoying. I would hope this would be fixed in an update as well as a seated mode. This needs to have a seated mode and a save system for sure. If you want to have a time limit that should be an option for those that don't want one we have to start the entire thing over again which becomes frustrating and makes me not want to go back. The fact that it is multiplayer and has good environmental rooms makes this still a must-have. Also it is way too short there are not enough rooms and I anticipate the devs will be adding more as we go. Other than that, I would definitely take advantage of any sales as it is certainly worth adding to your library if you're a fan of escape rooms and want to be able to play with your friends and family.

In response to the devs, there are people not lucky enough to be able to stand, a disability is not a reason to not enjoy a game. You need a seated mode which would put you at the height you would need to be to do anything in the game and a crouch mode if you need to go nearer to the floor. There is no VR game out there that has to be standing....and if you're basing it on a real escape room people would run out of time leave and never come back is what would happen this is not a real escape room.... it's VR

developer_response.body: Thank you for your interest in our game. The seatting mode you mentioned was an argumentative idea during development, but we didn't implement it because there are gimmicks that require you to stand up during the game. Also, we didn't include a save system because our concept is to recreate the experience of a real-life escape in VR. However, we're always listening to your feedback and will work on improving it.

reviews.review_description: We have only made it through the first two levels and wow is it fun. My wife and I play together and its just challenging enough to keep it interesting. Only reason its not a 5 is because the graphics could be better and the movements more refined. If I could id give it a 4.5!

I have not yet completed the game. Stuck on the Pharoah at the moment. I managed to avoid looking up cheats until now but it's become too hard for me now. I would be happy to keep working put the solutions but when there is a timer it just spoils the experience for me. I would rather take my time and get there eventually. It doesn't really add anything to the game by having the time limit. Also it is incredibly frustrating when you have worked through tricky puzzles and you have to start again when the clock runs out. This is particularly bad when you think you have completed a 'room' but end up on the same level in a 2nd, 3rd or even a 4th room as I am finding on the pharaoh level. If I run out of time on my next attempt I may end up just giving up the game, which would be a shame. Maybe have the option of a timer for competitive people and allow us plodder single-players to dawdle. Great fun though.
***Update.. sadly I have given up on the last room. I am not physically fit enough to pass the drumming 'puzzle' so I can't finish the game. To drum fast enough and for long enough is not possible for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing our game. As a developer, time limits can be a big help for retention because they can create a repetitive challenge for your game. This is because if you end play with a one-time experience, revisits may be limited but, we'll discuss lifting the time limit with the development team to make the game more fun.

reviews.review_title: Starts off great but steadily declines
reviews.review_description:
I'm always on the lookout for puzzle/escape room games.
This one starts off amazingly with the spooky Doll House, the puzzles are good and it has great atmosphere. Unfortunately, I found each subsequent room to be less interesting also there were a few times I hit the time limit which force you to restart. I don't see the value in this, sure if your paying for a physical escape room I get it but here your just making me waste my time by repeating steps I've already solved. Visually the game is perfectly serviceable but is in no way a graphical showcase. The developer has still done a good job here but it's definitely no where close to games like The Room A Dark Matter or The 7th Guest.

developer_response.body: Thank you for experiencing our game. We didn't allow save saves at launch to ensure a real life escape room experience, but we've listened to your requests and will be discussing save modes with our developers.

reviews.review_title: Tons of fun! Especially in multiplayer
reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed these puzzles.  There's not enough of them that allow multiplayer.
Love it a lot, wish there was more games or updates for new games will totally paid for extras games

The concept of a VR Co-Op Escape Room game is something that really excites me. My family and I love both Escape Rooms and VR puzzle games, so this should’ve been a match made in heaven - and in some ways It was. Five escape rooms of different concepts feels like great value for money, and we definitely found most of the puzzles to be a good level of challenge. 

However,  lots of small issues added up to a considerably less fun play experience. Here is a list of all the changes that we would make:

- Adjust the difficulty: Some specific puzzles were incredibly hard to complete within the time constraints. We have tried a large range of puzzle games and never struggled this much. This is the only puzzle game which required us to search for solutions online, and this happened on multiple occasions. Specific puzzles include the pipe step in the Secret Lab level and the Third stage of the Pharaoh Level. A good way to fix this would be to improve the hint system, as it is barely useful already.

- Allow players to collect the collectibles in multiplayer: The collectibles system is one we love a lot, it gives us more puzzles to complete and gives us a reason to come back and play the stages again. However, for some reason the collectibles don’t appear in your lobby when playing multiplayer. Seeing as this is marketed as a Co-Op Escape Room game this decision is genuinely baffling. We have absolutely no desire to return to the escape rooms and finish the other puzzles as there is zero reward for doing so. Please fix this.

- Add More Avatars: This is less of a complaint and more of a suggestion to improve. Customisability is important to keep players engaged and adding more avatars will help differentiate between the players and add a level of uniqueness to the gameplay.

- Movement speed needs to be improved: The teleportation movement works very well, but as someone who much prefers smooth moment, I feel constantly discouraged from using it. The movement speed is so unbelievably slow that you may aswell use teleportation, which to me breaks immersion. To fix this please add a moment speed adjuster, like most VR games have.

- Improve object tracking: Picking up objects in this game is not very good. The tracking is poorly optimised and more often than not it takes multiple attempts to properly grab an object. This sometimes goes as far as to completely deactivate one hand for the rest of the game, which is a glitch that definitely needs fixing as it has occurred multiple times.

Whilst I do believe fixing all of these issues would make the game substantially better, I believe my biggest complaint is that the game lacks personality. Whilst about three out of the five escape rooms are pretty good, I can’t help but shake the feeling that they were rushed. Not only are the textures constantly out of focus (which may be a glitch that needs fixing) but the general art style of the maps is very generic and does not work well with the Quest platform in my option. One thing that strengthens this case is the actual posters of the maps themselves. After analysing these posters they seem to have been generated using AI. Now I have no way to fully verify this, so If this is untrue I apologise to the artists, but I really do believe it to be true. Using generative AI to make these posters is not only incredibly disappointing and immoral, but also represents exactly what I feel about this game, which is a lack of personality.

To close this off, I understand I am being overly critical. I know how hard it is to make a game, and to the developers I appreciate you trying to make fun escape rooms, but there is a lot of work necessary to make this game brilliant in my opinion.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed feedback. 

1) Adjusting the difficulty: This is a subjective area, and some people find it very easy while others find it very difficult. Developers put a lot of thought into the questions and users will solve them based on their skill level. 

2) Collectibles in multiplayer mode: This issue was not implemented for technical reasons. We are aware of this issue and will be notifying players before multiplayer games. 

3) Adding avatars: At launch, we focused on game gimmicks rather than character development to maximize our limited resources. We're listening to player requests and if there are a lot of requests for this, we'll consider it.

4) Improved movement speed: At launch, there was only one way to move, teleportation, and we've added free movement based on user requests. We've been thinking about the right level of movement speed to avoid motion sickness, and if there are a lot of requests, we'll consider making changes. 

5) Improved object tracking: We'll discuss this with the developers to make it a better experience.

reviews.review_description: Challenging.  Loved it! Hoping for updates.
This is a great game, but... the timer should be optional, or the game continue through the timer... because it's the only janky thing that ruins the game play.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment. We didn't reflect the feature you mentioned because we wanted to reproduce Real Life Escape Room as similar as possible in VR. However, we're listening to user feedback and making improvements. :)

reviews.review_title: Great game, but no save facility
reviews.review_description:
Very atmospheric escape room with great graphics. However, when leaving the game  it does not auto save and I can find no way of saving it beforehand so every time I come back to it, I have to start from the beginning again.
any solutions. ???￼

developer_response.body:
Thank you for playing our game. 
We intentionally did not include an intermediate save mode at launch to provide a real life escape room experience. However, our developers are listening to your feedback and are discussing the issues you mentioned.

reviews.review_description: Wife and I are having a great time with this experience.  The only wish so far would be to add a more time or timeless feature.

The game looks and sounds great ascwell as seemingly having some nice puzzles overall (can't say personally), and it's truly a game I want to love (except for the SNAP TURNING with no option for Smooth turn.... in 2024. Which I absolutely HATE! Thank god they didn't force teleportation movement as well I guess...🙄)..
I'm also disappointed that there are only 5 rooms, all of which need to be completed in 1 session (seriously... That's fine for 10-15 minute rooms, but not demanding 40+ minute rooms that force you to make time without Any distractions in a day. Not easy for an adult to do..). So the games content, unless added to consistently- is lacking to me. 
All of that said, I can't love this game because on the very first room (The Doll Room), there is 1 "puzzle" very early in it that is less a puzzle and more reliant on random luck that's ONLY there to waste the time given to you for the room (as well as completely waste your real world time...); the SLIDER "PUZZLE"...
Try as I might, I can't get through it, and after 30 minutes, I don't WANT to get through it!
These are RIDICULOUS  additions to escape rooms or puzzle games in general, and akin to throwing in a mandatory Rubix Cube amongst the other puzzles SIMPLY TO WASTE YOUR TIME!!

Sadly there's not even a solution guide for this slider puzzle I can find anywhere, so I'm definitely just considering getting a refund at this point.
There's too many other puzzle games I have that give actual puzzles to waste any more time on a mandatory slider skill test/ clock waster...

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed feedback—it’s clear you put a lot of thought into your experience with the game, and we appreciate you sharing it. 🙏

We hear your frustrations regarding the slider puzzle and the overall time constraints. While puzzles like the slider are intended to add a layer of challenge, we understand they can feel more like barriers than fun problem-solving for some players. Your point about wasting time is valid, and we'll certainly take it into account as we review future puzzle designs. We'll also explore ways to provide better hints or solutions for tricky sections, so you're never left stuck without help.

Regarding snap turning and time-limited sessions, we've heard similar concerns from other players and are actively considering updates to address these, including adding smooth turning and potentially offering a way to tackle rooms across multiple sessions.

Finally, while the game currently includes five rooms, we are committed to expanding the content and ensuring a richer experience for our players over time. Your feedback is essential in shaping those future updates.

We hope you’ll consider revisiting the game once we’ve rolled out some of these improvements. Thank you again for your candid review—it helps us make the game better for everyone! 😊

reviews.review_description: If this has come out a year ago, I would have given it a 10/10 but there’s been a slew of new escape room games these last few months. My top 3 in order are: Escape Simulator, House of Da Vinci, then Another Room. The first 2 are GOTY contenders for sure. Again, that’s not to say this game isn’t worth the purchase. It is.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts and honest comparison! We're thrilled you found Another Door worth purchasing and appreciate being ranked alongside some fantastic titles. 😊

We’re continuously working to improve and innovate, so who knows—maybe Another Door can climb higher on your list in the future! If you have any specific suggestions or features you'd love to see, we’d be happy to hear them. Thanks again for playing! 🚪🧩

reviews.review_description: Cant even get past the tutorial, I dropped the key card and cannot pickup. Please do like most games that allow you to point at something in a certain range and it highlight for distance pickup.

developer_response.body: Thank you for experiencing our game. We'll pass your feedback on to our development team to look into.

reviews.review_description: Comparing this game on Quest 3 with ‘Rooms of Reality’ ,another escape room game, I instantly noticed that the textures look pixelated and kinda washed out, plus the avatars have no facial expressions and look very generic  - there are some minor issues with the controls too. I think at a minimum there is a graphics update needed. I was remembering the olden Quest 1 days when playing this game. No joke. The content though is good, and the puzzles are demanding. I’d still recommend this title if you don’t mind the lo res graphics.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your detailed feedback and for giving Another Door: Escape Room a try! We’re glad to hear you enjoyed the content and challenging puzzles – it’s great to know that part of our effort resonated with you. 😊

Regarding the graphics and avatars, we appreciate your honest critique. Improving visuals and enhancing the overall immersion is something we're actively exploring, especially with the power of newer hardware like Quest 3. Updates and optimizations are always a priority for us, and your input helps us focus on areas that matter most to players like you.

As for control issues, we’re continuously working on refinements and would love to hear more specifics so we can address them in future updates.

Thank you for your recommendation despite the concerns – it means a lot to us! Stay tuned for potential updates, and feel free to share any other suggestions. We’re always listening. 🙌

reviews.review_description: Been waiting for this for years!

It was a very organized and fun game. Each map had its own unique concept, and the graphics were impressive. If new maps are added in the future, I hope they include something on a scale that we could never experience in real life. It would also be helpful to have difficulty levels displayed for each map, so players know what to expect. Additionally, a mid-game save feature would be great… It’s challenging to start over if you have other tasks to attend to or your battery dies. I’m looking forward to the next release with fresh and exciting maps! I highly recommend it to everyone.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your thoughtful feedback! We're thrilled that you enjoyed the unique concepts and graphics of each map. Your ideas for larger-than-life maps and difficulty levels are fantastic and align well with what we aim to bring to VR escape rooms. We're also listening to all the requests for a mid-game save feature—making the game accessible and flexible is important to us, and we’ll look into how we can incorporate this in future updates. Stay tuned for the new maps in the works, and thank you for the recommendation!

reviews.review_description:
My husband and I love playing escape room games and this one is awesome!  There aren’t many multiplayer escape room games available so we were excited to try this one and it didn’t disappoint.
The puzzles are challenging which is great.  Hoping they come out with more rooms soon!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words! We're thrilled to hear that you and your husband had a great time with the game, especially as escape room fans! We’ve worked hard to make the puzzles challenging and engaging, so it’s awesome to know they hit the mark for you both. Stay tuned—more rooms are definitely in the works! 😊

reviews.review_title: This game is blast with friends
reviews.review_description: 😂The jump scares got me more than I’d like to admit! I’ve only escaped two of the scenarios so far and will be completing all the other ones with my group. I’m hoping we’ll see more content in the future (free content and dlc). This game is a blast. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback! We're thrilled to hear that you're enjoying the game and that the jump scares are making an impact! It’s great to know you're tackling all the scenarios with your group. We're definitely looking into more content for the future, including both free updates and DLC. Your support and recommendations mean a lot to us! Happy escaping!

reviews.review_description:
I love these types of game, and this one looked really good, however a "here we go again" moment when you invariably encounter a puzzle that's colour based and the developers haven't included a 'colourblind mode'. Yep, me and 25% of the population are utterly stuck on level 3. 

A real shame, as I was loving the game up until then!

Now is the time when you can enjoy high-quality room escape in VR.
The graphics are great, and the game is fun.
There were five themes, and the volume per theme was significant, so we had a rich play experience.
I hope other themes will be updated as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your wonderful review! We're thrilled to hear that you enjoyed the high-quality graphics and immersive gameplay in our VR escape room. Stay tuned for more exciting content in the future!

reviews.review_description: Room Escape Game VR is awesome. The graphics are great, the atmosphere is perfect, and the design is awesome. It was a satisfying purchase.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the wonderful feedback! We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed the graphics, atmosphere, and design—it's our goal to make every detail as immersive and enjoyable as possible. So glad you found it a satisfying purchase! If you ever have any thoughts on features or content you'd like to see, we’d love to hear them. Happy escaping!

reviews.review_title: Need a friend to play with
reviews.review_description:
So scared to do it alone..

I was playing and the doll popped out... 
Almost threw the controller for a moment.

It's a shame that single mode only has scary things. 
It would be nice to have friends.

developer_response.body: Pharaoh's Curse and Altar of Sun are also single-play available.

reviews.review_title: A challenging escape game with friends
reviews.review_description: I play this game with my friends. I don't like horror series, so we are playing an adventure scenario called 'Altar of the Sun.' This scenario resembles a historical site of the Maya civilization. There are a few puzzle games with no hints. We are trying to solve the puzzles using trial and error. We discuss the puzzles many times and feel really good when we solve them. We have solved the first stage. I feel weird in the second stage because there are a lot of meat and objects. We can't solve this stage due to a lack of batteries. We have been playing for over an hour. This game is very fun and difficult, even thogh I play with my friends. However, the warp movement method is inconvenient.

developer_response.body: We'll update it for a better gaming experience, Thank you.

reviews.review_description: The process of deducing strange puzzles and reaching the results is fun. The dark environment description is also excellent, and if you are looking for a unique VR game, it is recommended to play it.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your thoughtful feedback! We hope to continue providing engaging content that keeps players like you coming back for more!

reviews.review_description: There is no re-entry in co-op mode if the connection is lost. Please be sure to fix this!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback! We understand how important re-entry is for a seamless co-op experience, and our team is actively looking into options to improve this feature. In the meantime, if you encounter any connection issues, please reach out to our support team or join our Discord community for assistance. We’re committed to enhancing the game and really appreciate your patience!

reviews.review_description: I like horror games, but it was more interesting than any other game that comes out these days. It's easy to control and the fun of escaping the room while looking for clues is very good. If you play games alone at night, you can feel extreme fear.

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you are having fun. Make sure to check out all of the episodes, they are quite different!

reviews.review_title: I saw people who were scared while playi
reviews.review_description: I bought this game after watching a YouTube short, and it was funny to see people freaking out. But then I found myself freaking out, and it was so scary

developer_response.body: Another Door: Escape Room includes not only scary episodes, but also episodes where you can become Indiana Jones and explore the Egyptian pyramids and the altar of the sun! Enjoy your game!

reviews.review_description: I usually like escape room content. This game is 100% fulfilling and fun. The difficulty level was just right and it was a lot of fun playing cooperatively with friends :) I recommend it to many people :)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your positive feedback! We’re delighted to hear that you enjoyed the game and found it fulfilling. It's fantastic that the cooperative aspect added to your experience with friends. Your recommendation means a lot to us! We hope you continue to have fun with our escape room content!
